title: Import a coded-long CSV, run HB-Stan sampling, and finalize in one call
summary: Import a coded-long CSV, run HB-MNL sampling via CmdStan, and immediately finalize to produce diagnostics and part-worth exports — all in one call. Equivalent to running fit then finalize back-to-back with a single started/finished event pair.
description: Convenience wrapper that runs the full `fit → finalize` sequence in a single command: converts the coded-long CSV to Stan JSON, runs NUTS sampling, then computes Rhat/ESS diagnostics, posterior summaries, and the part-worth matrix. All intermediate files (stan_data.json, chain CSVs, diagnostics.json, summary.json, part_worths.csv) are saved in run_dir. Use this when you want the complete deliverable in one step; use `fit` + `finalize` separately when you need to inspect raw chains.
usage:
[["hb-stan::fit-finalize"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "stan_run"
# optional:
headers = true
num_samples = 1000
num_warmup = 500
num_chains = 4
seed = <int>
thin = <int>
parallel_chains = true
prior_lkj_eta = 1.0
sigma_scale = 1.0
ridge_scale = 1e-6

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV. Required columns: resp_id, task_id, alt_id, x1, ..., xP, y. Rows must be sorted ascending by (resp_id, task_id, alt_id).

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. Receives stan_data.json, chain CSV files, diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default true.

arguments.name: thin
arguments.description: Thinning factor. Omit to keep all draws.

arguments.name: parallel_chains
arguments.description: Run chains in parallel. Default true.

arguments.name: prior_lkj_eta
arguments.description: LKJ prior shape parameter η. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: sigma_scale
arguments.description: Half-normal prior scale for individual σ. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: ridge_scale
arguments.description: Ridge scale for covariance prior. Default 1e-6.

arguments.name: covariate_input
arguments.description: Path to a CSV file with respondent-level covariates. First column must be resp_id (case-insensitive) matching UIDs in the training CSV. Remaining columns are numeric covariates. An intercept column of 1s is prepended automatically. Omit for intercept-only model.

details:
`fit-finalize` is the one-shot workflow command. It converts the coded-long CSV to Stan JSON, runs NUTS sampling, then immediately computes diagnostics, posterior summaries, and part-worths — all within a single protocol envelope, producing one started/finished event pair. All intermediate files are preserved in run_dir.

Use the two-step form (`fit` then `finalize`) when you want to inspect the raw chain CSVs first, or when you plan to re-run `finalize` multiple times.
value:
On success, run_dir contains the full set of fit and finalize outputs:

- stan_data.json, hb_fit_chain_*.csv (from the fit step).
- diagnostics.json, summary.json, part_worths.csv (from the finalize step).

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, progress events during sampling, then event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: To re-finalize after the fact, run `finalize` directly on run_dir — the chain CSVs are preserved.
Rows in the input CSV must be sorted by (resp_id, task_id, alt_id).
CmdStan must be installed and on PATH.

see_also.description: Sampling-only step — use when you want to inspect chains before finalizing.
Cross-validation counterpart — runs cv-fit then cv-finalize in one call.

examples.title: One-shot fit and finalize with defaults
Longer sampling run with reproducible seed
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::fit-finalize"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-long"
run_name = "stan_run2"
num_samples = 2000
num_warmup = 1000
seed = 42

examples.title: Tight prior for a sparse study
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::fit-finalize"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-tight"
run_name = "stan_run3"
prior_lkj_eta = 4.0
sigma_scale = 0.5
seed = 7

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: thin
fields.description: Keep every Nth draw. Omit for no thinning.

fields.name: parallel_chains
fields.description: Run chains in parallel. Default: true.

fields.name: prior_lkj_eta
fields.description: LKJ prior shape parameter η. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: sigma_scale
fields.description: Half-normal prior scale for individual σ. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: ridge_scale
fields.description: Ridge scale for covariance prior. Default: 1e-6.

fields.name: covariate_input
fields.description: Path to respondent-level covariate CSV. Omit for intercept-only.

artifacts.title: Stan data file
artifacts.description: Converted Stan JSON for reproducibility. Use with the sample command to re-run sampling with different settings without repeating data conversion.

artifacts.title: Raw posterior chain draws
artifacts.description: One CmdStan CSV per chain — retained so finalize can be re-run without repeating sampling.

artifacts.title: Convergence diagnostics
artifacts.description: Per-parameter Rhat and ESS. Rhat above 1.01 indicates poor chain mixing — re-run with more samples or warmup.

artifacts.title: Posterior parameter summary
artifacts.description: Population-level posterior mean, SD, and quantiles for all model parameters.

artifacts.title: Individual part-worth utilities
artifacts.description: The key output file. One row per respondent, one column per attribute. Posterior-mean utilities for each respondent. Feed to sim_db_build (model_type=hb_stan) to create a market simulator.

fields.name: (attribute columns)
fields.description: Posterior-mean utility per attribute per respondent.
